results.title: tina broad peel tether repair
results.prompt: Repair the first reference into a more physically legible photoreal candy-commercial styleframe. Its lower end remains visibly attached to a raw lifted edge on the car roof; the same uninterrupted band curls once through the air and is taut in Tina's raised hand. results.title: tina neighborhood is a moving stage set
results.prompt: Create a photoreal cinematic commercial styleframe answering Ben's question “what is outside Tina's window?” without using peeling, portals, or gummy material. Preserve the same Tina, wardrobe, pink Lychee Peelerz and package, and ordinary rideshare interior. Seen through her open passenger window, the entire suburban neighborhood is revealed as a shallow practical theater set being moved past the stationary car: full-size painted house facades ride on visible wheeled tracks, a blue-sky cloth backdrop hangs from a rig, and two bored teenage stagehands in everyday school clothes quietly push the scenery. Tina has just bitten the candy and watches, amused but unsurprised. Make the window frame the boundary between real car and practical set. One impossible reveal, grounded daylight, tactile production design, no fantasy glow, no peel, no candyland, no readable signs, no watermark.

results.title: couple campus rotates around them control

The couple remains perfectly upright and weightless, facing one another, while gravity has rotated the campus ninety degrees around them: students calmly walk along the vertical stone wall as though it were pavement, bicycles rest against the former ground overhead, and loose leaves fall sideways toward the building.
